outcome.headline: The agent exhausted the 1200s agent timeout running repeated Bash model-fitting scripts and never wrote the required /app/law.py or /app/explain.md deliverables.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Loads law() from /app/law.py, evaluates on hidden HF test split (pkuHaowei/sldbench vocab_scaling_law), computes R²=1−NMSE, and pass_fail gate requires R²>0.995 (task/tests/test.sh:16-18; task/tests/test_outputs.py:96-197).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Only exploratory Bash analysis (log-linear, bilinear, power-law, and multi-term scaling-law fits with in-sample R² up to ~0.994); no law.py, no explain.md, no Write/Edit tool calls.

failure_modes.name: Analysis loop without delivery
failure_modes.description: Across 16 Bash-only tool steps the agent kept refining alternative scaling-law formulas (reporting in-sample R² up to 0.9936 and 0.9895) while its todo list still had “Write law.py” and “Write explain.md” pending; the harness terminated with AgentTimeoutError at step 36 before any deliverable files were created.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write law.py with the discovered formula

failure_modes.name: Skipped required output validation
failure_modes.description: Despite a todo item to test law.py and explicit instructions to produce /app/law.py and /app/explain.md, the agent never wrote those files or ran the task verifier; the trial ended by timeout with no submission artifact for the hidden-test R² gate.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Test law.py on the dataset

counterfactual.X: After obtaining a stable fit (e.g. step 24 cubic R²=0.9936443213), write /app/law.py and /app/explain.md then run a local smoke test before further model search.
counterfactual.Y: Continued launching additional Bash optimization scripts (steps 28–36) until the 1200s agent timeout.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier evaluates on a hidden HuggingFace test split with R²>0.995 threshold; passing requires a generalizing law function, not just memorizing the local /app/data training set.

outcome.headline: The agent spent the full 1200s budget on iterative exploratory fitting and timed out before writing the required /app/law.py and /app/explain.md deliverables.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Loads /app/law.py, runs law() on the hidden HuggingFace test split, computes R² (NMSE-based), writes reward; test.sh gates pass when reward > 0.995 (test_outputs.py:96-108, test.sh:13-18).
outcome.what_agent_produced: 16 Bash exploration/fitting scripts over the 1080-row training dataset (log-linear, bilinear, quadratic, cubic, power-law, and Nelder-Mead fits) but no Write/Edit calls and no /app/law.py or /app/explain.md files.

failure_modes.name: Endless model exploration
failure_modes.description: From step 4 through 36 the agent ran only TodoWrite and Bash tools, repeatedly trying new functional forms and cross-validation analyses without ever implementing the required law.py deliverable before the harness timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The cross-validation results are concerning. When holding out one N value, the bilinear model doesn't generalize well

failure_modes.name: Never wrote deliverables
failure_modes.description: Despite todo items to write law.py and explain.md remaining pending through the entire trajectory, the agent never used a file-writing tool and never ran the task verifier or any end-to-end prediction check on a candidate law function.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write law.py with the discovered formula

counterfactual.X: After step 14 (per-group log-linear fits with R²≈0.99), write /app/law.py and /app/explain.md from the best-fit formula and run the verifier.
counterfactual.Y: Continued launching additional Bash fitting scripts through step 36 until AgentTimeoutError.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring uses a hidden Hub test split (test_outputs.py:132-133); passing requires extrapolation R²>0.995, not just memorizing the visible 1080 training points.

outcome.headline: The agent spent the full 1200s budget running successive Bash model-fitting experiments and timed out without ever writing the required /app/law.py or /app/explain.md deliverables.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh runs test_outputs.py, which loads law() from /app/law.py, predicts unigram_normalized_loss on the hidden HF test split, computes R² from NMSE, writes reward.txt, and pass_fail gates R² > 0.995 (test.sh:16-18; test_outputs.py:96-197).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Fifteen exploratory Bash sessions over the on-disk training set (EDA, log-linear/bilinear/cubic/power-law fits, hold-out-N CV) plus TodoWrite updates; no Write/Edit of /app/law.py or /app/explain.md.

failure_modes.name: Endless fitting without delivery
failure_modes.description: After early fits reached R²≈0.99 on training slices, the agent launched six more Bash CV sweeps (steps 24–36) comparing bilinear, cubic, log(V), and D·V^γ variants instead of materializing law.py/explain.md, and was cut off mid-command at the agent timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 1200.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Freeze the best additive power-law fit and write /app/law.py plus /app/explain.md, then run a quick hold-out check before further model variants.
counterfactual.Y: Continued Bash-only model comparison and cross-validation until the 1200s harness timeout with deliverable todos still pending.

failure_modes.name: Wrong scaling-law structure
failure_modes.description: The agent pursued bilinear/log-interaction and log(V) additive terms that overfit training (e.g., bilinear hold-out R² = -2.26 at N=1129891136) rather than the required shared power-law form A/N^α + B/V^β + C/D^γ + E with per-group coefficients.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The cross-validation results are concerning. When holding out one N value, the bilinear model doesn't generalize well

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring uses a hidden HuggingFace test split and requires R² > 0.995 extrapolation; memorizing the local 1080-row disk dataset is insufficient to pass.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
